pages.full_text: On we went to Avila. The heat caught up with us, so we didn't climb the walls. We saw the cathedral. It has a beautiful alabaster altar, but it is roped off so you can't see it. There were more nice alabaster carvings in the musean which we could see.
pages.notes: Diary spells 'Avila' without accent in text (postcard label reads 'ÁVILA'). 'musean' appears to be the diarist's spelling of 'museum'. The postcard of Ávila is glued in; text continues to the right of the postcard. No handwritten page number visible.

pages.full_text:
We drove on to a campsite which is as well run as the last one was badly run. The man at the accuil wasn't very good at communicating + didn't finish interviews properly. He just expected people to realise they should go + look for their own site. He gave me the key to the ladies' disable toilet, where the washing machine was. I did a wash + we went for a swim in the lovely [?lake]. Our pitch is near the chemical toilet with a small gate leading out to a rocky path + down to the lake.

    The camp is packed cheek-by-jowl. People from Madrid with their microwaves + fridges in rented caravans. Cigarette smoke + [strikethrough] rock music + computer games. Very friendly + there's a baby Justin's age nearby, with his toddler brother [strikethrough: that] whom I've pushed on his bike. No shade, so Simon's put up the awning. He's had to mend the water tank strap that ripped off on its way down here,
pages.notes: 'accuil' is likely the diarist's spelling of French 'accueil' (reception/welcome desk). The word after 'lovely' before the line continues is partly obscured; reads as 'lake' from context. One word is struck through before 'rock music'. 'that' is struck through and replaced with 'whom'. Text appears to continue onto the next page. No handwritten page number visible. 'Justin' appears to be a family member (child) used as an age reference.
